concepts.name: Data Structure
concepts.description: A way of organizing and storing data to enable efficient access and modification

concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.description: A finite sequence of well-defined instructions to solve a problem

concepts.name: Abstract Data Type
concepts.description: A mathematical model defining data and operations independent of implementation

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.description: A method where the solution depends on solutions to smaller instances of the same problem

concepts.name: Iteration
concepts.description: Repeated execution of a set of instructions using loops

concepts.name: Pointer
concepts.description: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable

concepts.name: Node
concepts.description: A fundamental unit of a data structure that contains data and links to other nodes

concepts.name: Memory Allocation
concepts.description: The process of reserving memory space for data storage

concepts.name: Dynamic Memory
concepts.description: Memory allocated at runtime from the heap

concepts.name: Static Memory
concepts.description: Memory allocated at compile time with fixed size

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.description: The amount of time an algorithm takes as a function of input size

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.description: The amount of memory an algorithm uses as a function of input size

concepts.name: O(1)
concepts.description: Constant time complexity

concepts.name: O(log n)
concepts.description: Logarithmic time complexity

concepts.name: O(n)
concepts.description: Linear time complexity

concepts.name: O(n log n)
concepts.description: Linearithmic time complexity

concepts.name: O(n²)
concepts.description: Quadratic time complexity

concepts.name: Big-O Notation
concepts.description: Asymptotic notation describing the upper bound of an algorithm's growth rate

concepts.name: Array
concepts.description: A contiguous block of memory storing elements of the same type, accessed by index

concepts.name: Dynamic Array
concepts.description: An array that automatically resizes when capacity is exceeded

concepts.name: Index
concepts.description: An integer position used to access elements in an array

concepts.name: Linked List
concepts.description: A linear data structure where elements are connected via pointers

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.description: A linked list where each node has pointers to both next and previous nodes

concepts.name: Circular Linked List
concepts.description: A linked list where the last node points back to the first node

concepts.name: Head
concepts.description: The first node in a linked list

concepts.name: Tail
concepts.description: The last node in a linked list

concepts.name: Stack
concepts.description: A LIFO data structure where elements are added and removed from the top

concepts.name: Push
concepts.description: Add an element to the top of a stack

concepts.name: Pop
concepts.description: Remove and return the top element of a stack

concepts.name: Peek
concepts.description: View the top element without removing it

concepts.name: LIFO
concepts.description: Last In, First Out — the element added last is removed first

concepts.name: Stack Overflow
concepts.description: Error when pushing to a full stack

concepts.name: Stack Underflow
concepts.description: Error when popping from an empty stack

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.description: A FIFO data structure where elements are added at rear and removed from front

concepts.name: Enqueue
concepts.description: Add an element to the rear of a queue

concepts.name: Dequeue
concepts.description: Remove and return the front element of a queue

concepts.name: FIFO
concepts.description: First In, First Out — the element added first is removed first

concepts.name: Priority Queue
concepts.description: A queue where elements are dequeued based on priority rather than order

concepts.name: Circular Queue
concepts.description: A queue implemented using a circular array to efficiently use space

concepts.name: Deque
concepts.description: A double-ended queue allowing insertion and deletion at both ends

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.description: A hierarchical data structure with a root node and child nodes forming subtrees

concepts.name: Binary Tree
concepts.description: A tree where each node has at most two children (left and right)

concepts.name: Binary Search Tree
concepts.description: A binary tree where left child < parent < right child for all nodes

concepts.name: AVL Tree
concepts.description: A self-balancing BST where the height difference between subtrees is at most 1

concepts.name: Red-Black Tree
concepts.description: A self-balancing BST with color-coded nodes ensuring O(log n) operations

concepts.name: Heap
concepts.description: A complete binary tree satisfying the heap property (max-heap or min-heap)

concepts.name: Max Heap
concepts.description: A heap where every parent node is greater than or equal to its children

concepts.name: Min Heap
concepts.description: A heap where every parent node is less than or equal to its children

concepts.name: B-Tree
concepts.description: A self-balancing tree optimized for systems that read/write large blocks of data

concepts.name: Trie
concepts.description: A tree-like structure for storing strings where each node represents a character

concepts.name: Root
concepts.description: The topmost node in a tree with no parent

concepts.name: Leaf
concepts.description: A node with no children in a tree

concepts.name: Parent
concepts.description: A node that has one or more child nodes

concepts.name: Child
concepts.description: A node connected below its parent node

concepts.name: Subtree
concepts.description: A tree formed by a node and all its descendants

concepts.name: Tree Height
concepts.description: The length of the longest path from the root to a leaf

concepts.name: Balanced Tree
concepts.description: A tree where the height difference between subtrees is bounded

concepts.name: Inorder Traversal
concepts.description: Tree traversal visiting left subtree, root, then right subtree

concepts.name: Preorder Traversal
concepts.description: Tree traversal visiting root, left subtree, then right subtree

concepts.name: Postorder Traversal
concepts.description: Tree traversal visiting left subtree, right subtree, then root

concepts.name: Level Order Traversal
concepts.description: Tree traversal visiting nodes level by level from top to bottom

concepts.name: Tree Traversal
concepts.description: The process of visiting all nodes in a tree in a specific order

concepts.name: Graph
concepts.description: A collection of vertices connected by edges

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.description: A graph where edges have a direction from source to destination

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.description: A graph where edges have no direction

concepts.name: Weighted Graph
concepts.description: A graph where edges have associated weights or costs

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.description: A fundamental unit in a graph

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.description: A connection between two vertices in a graph

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.description: Graph representation using a list of neighbors for each vertex

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.description: Graph representation using a 2D matrix of edge weights

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.description: Graph traversal exploring all neighbors at the current depth before going deeper

concepts.name: Depth-First Search
concepts.description: Graph traversal exploring as far as possible along each branch before backtracking

concepts.name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
concepts.description: Finds the shortest path from a source to all other vertices in a weighted graph

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.description: Linear ordering of vertices such that for every directed edge u→v, u comes before v

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Tree
concepts.description: A spanning tree with the minimum total edge weight

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.description: The path between two vertices with the minimum total edge weight

concepts.name: Cycle
concepts.description: A path that starts and ends at the same vertex

concepts.name: Connected Graph
concepts.description: A graph where there is a path between every pair of vertices

concepts.name: Hash Table
concepts.description: A data structure that maps keys to values using a hash function

concepts.name: Hash Function
concepts.description: A function that maps data of arbitrary size to fixed-size values

concepts.name: Collision
concepts.description: When two different keys map to the same hash table index

concepts.name: Chaining
concepts.description: Collision resolution by storing multiple elements at the same index using a linked list

concepts.name: Open Addressing
concepts.description: Collision resolution by probing for the next empty slot

concepts.name: Load Factor
concepts.description: The ratio of stored elements to the total number of slots in a hash table

concepts.name: Sorting
concepts.description: The process of arranging elements in a specific order

concepts.name: Bubble Sort
concepts.description: A simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly swaps adjacent elements that are in the wrong order

concepts.name: Selection Sort
concepts.description: Sorting by repeatedly finding the minimum element and placing it at the beginning

concepts.name: Insertion Sort
concepts.description: Sorting by building a sorted portion one element at a time

concepts.name: Merge Sort
concepts.description: A divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm that recursively splits and merges subarrays

concepts.name: Quick Sort
concepts.description: A divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm using a pivot element to partition the array

concepts.name: Heap Sort
concepts.description: Sorting by building a heap and repeatedly extracting the maximum element

concepts.name: Comparison Sort
concepts.description: A sorting algorithm that determines order by comparing elements

concepts.name: Stable Sort
concepts.description: A sort that preserves the relative order of equal elements

concepts.name: Divide and Conquer
concepts.description: A strategy that breaks a problem into smaller subproblems, solves them, and combines results

concepts.name: Searching
concepts.description: The process of finding a specific element in a data structure

concepts.name: Linear Search
concepts.description: Searching by checking each element sequentially

concepts.name: Binary Search
concepts.description: Searching a sorted array by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.description: Adding a new element to a data structure

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.description: Removing an element from a data structure

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.description: Visiting all elements in a data structure

concepts.name: Access
concepts.description: Retrieving an element from a data structure

relationships.description: Each element is stored in a node
Has both next and prev pointers
BFS uses a queue for level-order exploration
DFS uses a stack (or call stack) for backtracking
Dijkstra uses a priority queue for greedy selection
Heap sort builds and uses a heap
Priority queues are commonly implemented with heaps
Hash tables use arrays as underlying storage
Binary search requires a sorted array
Stack follows Last In First Out
Queue follows First In First Out
BST may or may not be balanced
Heap is a complete binary tree
Linked list insertion at head is O(1)
Insertion sort is O(n²) worst case
Merge sort is O(n log n)
Quick sort average is O(n log n)
Heap sort is O(n log n)
Hash table operations are O(1) average
BFS explores breadth-first, DFS explores depth-first
